h2h_summary: Portugal and DR Congo have never previously met in international football, making their World Cup 2026 Group K opener the first ever encounter between the two nations.
team_style_home: Portugal play a possession-heavy 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 under Roberto Martinez, dominating midfield with Vitinha, Fernandes, and Bernardo Silva, using inverted full-backs and wide pace through Leao and Conceicao to break down compact defences.
team_style_away: DR Congo under Sebastien Desabre deploy a disciplined 4-2-3-1 or 4-4-2 built on a compact defensive block and rapid counter-attacks, leveraging Wissa's pace and direct running to exploit transitions.
wc_history_home: Portugal have appeared in the World Cup seven consecutive times, with their best finishes being third place in 1966 (led by Eusebio) and a fourth-place finish in 2006, but they have never won the tournament.
wc_history_away: DR Congo (then known as Zaire) made their only previous World Cup appearance in 1974 as the first Sub-Saharan African team in the tournament, losing all three group-stage matches before a 52-year absence ended with their 2026 qualification.
group_context: Group K features Portugal as heavy favourites alongside Colombia, DR Congo, and Uzbekistan; Portugal's opening match against DR Congo is crucial for establishing early momentum, while DR Congo — returning to the World Cup after 52 years — face immediate pressure to take points against a top European side to remain in contention for a historic knockout-stage run.
strengths_home: World-class midfield depth with Vitinha, Bruno Fernandes, and Bernardo Silva controlling tempo and creating chances
Dangerous wide attackers in Rafael Leao and Conceicao providing pace and direct threat down both flanks
Experienced and proven squad including Cristiano Ronaldo with 130+ international goals, backed by 2025 Nations League title
strengths_away: Organised and physically intense defensive structure under Desabre that frustrates attacking sides
Deadly counter-attacking threat through Yoane Wissa's pace and Bakambu's experienced finishing
High motivation and collective unity as a historic squad returning to the World Cup after 52 years with nothing to lose
